content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.3-codex-spark-xhigh

## Summary

I reviewed the current diff and key implementation files end-to-end. I found one high-confidence issue: non-linux PID liveness is effectively non-functional because records are marked non-authoritative, which can misclassify live CLI runs and break `run stop`/`run resume` behavior outside Linux. No other high-confidence correctness bugs were identified in these paths.

## Amendments

1. `run.pid` liveness handling in `src/adapters/fs.rs` is Linux-only authoritative on the `proc_start_ticks` field. `write_pid_file` sets `proc_start_ticks` only from `proc_start_ticks(pid)` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:291`), and on non-linux Unix that helper always returns `None` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:700`); `pid_record_is_authoritative` requires this field and `is_pid_alive` returns `false` when absent (`src/adapters/fs.rs:337`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:344`). As a result, on macOS/other non-linux Unix targets all records become non-authoritative, so `classify_running_snapshot_liveness` cannot return `CliProcess` (`src/cli/run.rs:1533`) and live runs are treated as stale, preventing reliable `run stop` signalling and causing resume/status to auto-fail live runs as stale.
   Rationale: This is a correctness and robustness gap in cross-platform behavior for process liveness, directly affecting stop/resume/stale detection for non-linux builds.
